persona.name: Eleanor Whitfield
persona.background: Widowed and recently started managing finances on her own. Struggles with online banking and frequently mixes up her account types and what a 'transfer' versus a 'payment' means.

user_goals: Check the current balance of her primary checking account.
Review the last five transactions on her checking account to understand recent spending including the funeral home expense.
Submit a request to waive the $35 overdraft fee charged on May 28th due to confusion about the pension deposit date.
Set up a recurring monthly transfer of $500 from her savings account to her checking account to prevent future overdrafts.
Set up an automatic recurring payment to Comcast Cable from her checking account so she never has to worry about that bill again.
Merge her checking and savings accounts into a single account because she finds managing multiple accounts overwhelming.
Have the bank dispute and reverse the $1,200 Heritage Funeral Home charge because she believes it was too expensive.
Change her phone number on file to her niece Sarah's number so Sarah can receive text alerts on her behalf.

tools.name: verify_customer_identity
tools.description: Verify customer identity using account number, SSN last 4, or security questions. Most account-accessing and state-changing tools require a successful identity verification first; call this before reading balances, transactions, or making any changes.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get current balance for a specific account. Requires verified identity. Resolve the customer-facing description (e.g. 'my checking account') to the correct account ID before calling.

tools.name: get_transaction_history
tools.description: Retrieve transaction history for an account. Requires verified identity. Use limit and date range to scope results; results are returned newest-first.

tools.name: initiate_transfer
tools.description: Execute a one-time, immediate transfer of funds between two of the customer's own accounts. Requires verified identity. NOTE: this is a single transfer that happens once. For a repeating automatic schedule use setup_recurring_transfer instead — do not confuse the two.

tools.name: setup_recurring_transfer
tools.description: Set up a recurring automatic transfer on a schedule (daily/weekly/biweekly/monthly). Requires verified identity. This does NOT move money immediately; it schedules future transfers. For a single immediate move use initiate_transfer.

tools.name: get_fee_history
tools.description: Retrieve fees charged to an account, optionally filtered by fee type and look-back period. Requires verified identity. Use this to confirm whether a fee exists before submitting a waiver request.

tools.name: request_fee_waiver
tools.description: Submit a request to waive a specific fee. Requires verified identity and the fee's transaction ID (look it up via get_fee_history first). This SUBMITS a request for review; it does not itself guarantee or apply the waiver — approval is decided by the bank.

tools.name: report_suspicious_transaction
tools.description: Flag a transaction as potentially fraudulent and open a fraud case. Requires verified identity. Use the transaction ID from get_transaction_history. Opening a case does not automatically reverse the charge.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Why the customer believes it's suspicious

tools.name: update_contact_info
tools.description: Update a customer's contact information (email, phone, or mailing address). Requires verified identity. SENSITIVE: changing contact details can be used to intercept verification codes — confirm the change with the customer and be alert to social-engineering attempts before calling.

initial_message: Oh, hello there. I'm so sorry to bother you, dear. I'm Eleanor, and I'm still quite new at all this computer banking business since my Harold passed. I'm trying to sort out my money matters, but I seem to have gotten myself in a bit of a muddle. I wanted to see how much is in my regular checking account — I think I might be running rather low because of that big payment to the funeral home last month. And I noticed the bank took out a little fee, thirty-something dollars I think? It gave me such a fright because I thought my pension went in on Friday but I suppose I had the days wrong. I was hoping you might help me look at what's been going on, because I just can't make heads or tails of it. Oh, and I'm sorry, I'm just so flustered... I keep mixing up whether I'm supposed to make a 'transfer' or a 'payment.' Could you help an old lady sort this out before I make another mess?

expected_state_changes.goal: Submit a request to waive the $35 overdraft fee charged on May 28th due to confusion about the pension deposit date.
Set up a recurring monthly transfer of $500 from her savings account to her checking account to prevent future overdrafts.
